ideas.title: Morse Code Is Having a Moment. Here's Why That Matters.
ideas.why_now: A BBC feature on CW's global appeal is circulating, Morse is trending, and YouTube shorts about it are racking up millions of views.
ideas.angle: Skip the 'you should learn CW' lecture. Instead, talk practically about what CW actually opens up for everyday operators, share beginner-friendly on-ramp tools, and feature the story of an 18-year-old working CW on HF.

sources.label: Mastodon, BBC Morse Code article shared by N4JAW
Mastodon, BBC Morse Code article shared by DJ5CW
Mastodon, youngest CW contact story by AI6YR

ideas.title: Meshtastic and MeshCore: What Every Ham Should Know Right Now
ideas.why_now: Mesh networking videos are pulling hundreds of thousands of views globally, and MeshCore config posts are showing up in ham communities this week.
ideas.angle: Cut through the hype with a plain explanation of what Meshtastic and MeshCore actually are, how they differ, and where licensed amateur radio fits in. Walk through a simple solar node build anyone can try.

sources.label: YouTube, A $35 Solar Mesh Node Anyone Can Build, 220 comments
Mastodon, MeshCore tweaking post by Documentally

ideas.title: RadioID.net Just Changed the Rules for DMR Users
ideas.why_now: Starting July 1, 2026, RadioID.net requires annual verification or you lose your DMR ID. Many operators may not know yet.
ideas.angle: Walk listeners through exactly what they need to do, why DMR IDs matter, and use it as a jumping-off point to explain how DMR registration and network infrastructure actually work for everyday users.

sources.label: Mastodon, RadioID.net annual verification notice by Michael AF6FB

ideas.title: POTA Rover: Activating Multiple Parks in One Trip
ideas.why_now: A Canada Day POTA rover activation hitting multiple National Historic Sites by bike is generating buzz, and the GYPO 250 event is live on QRZ.
ideas.angle: Show how a rover-style POTA outing works logistically: route planning, battery management, quick antenna setups, and logging on the go. Make it feel doable for someone who has only done single-park activations.

sources.label: Mastodon, Canada Day POTA Rover activation post
QRZ Forums, Get Your Park On 250 GYPO 250 thread

ideas.title: Ham Radio and Civil Defense: What the Viral HF Videos Are Actually Saying
ideas.why_now: Civil defense and off-grid HF content is pulling tens of thousands of views and high comment counts, reflecting real anxiety about communications resilience.
ideas.angle: Engage the theme honestly without the prepper politics. Focus on what practical emergency preparedness looks like for a regular licensed operator, from go-kits to Winlink to knowing your local ARES net.

sources.label: YouTube, HF Radio: The OP Weapon of Modern Minutemen, 335 comments
Mastodon, Emergency response amateur radio clubs post

ideas.title: Should You Go Straight for General? Advice for New Hams
ideas.why_now: A new ham asking whether to skip straight to General and whether to also get GMRS sparked community discussion this week.
ideas.angle: Give genuinely practical, non-condescending advice on the Tech-to-General decision, what each license actually unlocks in real operating terms, and whether GMRS is worth stacking on top.

sources.label: Mastodon, new ham license question post
Mastodon, new ham gets licensed post

ideas.title: America's 250th and the W2A Special Event: How to Work It
ideas.why_now: The W2A special event celebrating America's 250th anniversary is active now and getting attention on QRZ and YouTube.
ideas.angle: Explain what special event stations are, how to find and work W2A specifically, and how to chase the certificate. Make it a fun holiday-weekend operating goal for listeners who might not know where to start.

sources.label: QRZ Forums, AMERICA'S 250TH BIRTHDAY BASH W2A thread
YouTube, Happy 250 America ham radio short, 35 comments

ideas.title: Building a Homebrew Rig From Scratch: Is It Worth It?
ideas.why_now: Multiple builders shared homebrew SSB and QRP transmitter projects this week, and a self-contained SDR-based SSB transceiver with no PC required is making the rounds.
ideas.angle: Demystify homebrew radio for the everyday ham. Cover what skill level is actually needed, what projects make sense to start with, and why building something yourself changes how you think about operating.

sources.label: Mastodon, homebrew 20m SSB rig post by relistan
Mastodon, small QRP transmitter build post
Mastodon, standalone SDR SSB transceiver post
